steps.title: Compute each power
steps.content: Reduce the angle mod $\pi$ since $\tan$ has period $\pi$: $$\tfrac{92\pi}{3}=30\pi+\tfrac{2\pi}{3}\equiv\tfrac{2\pi}{3}.$$

steps.title: Add and identify
steps.content: So $\tan\tfrac{92\pi}{3}=\tan\tfrac{2\pi}{3}=-\sqrt{3}$. That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. A trigonometric ratio can be found using the unit circle, that is, a circle with radius $1$ unit. If a central angle of a unit circle in the xy-plane centered at the origin has its starting side on the positive x-axis and its terminal side intersects the circle at a point $\left(x,y\right)$, then the value of the tangent of the central angle is equal to the y-coordinate divided by the x-coordinate. There are $2π$ radians in a circle. Dividing $\frac{92π}{3}$ by $2π$ yields $\frac{92}{6}$, which is equivalent to $15+\frac{2}{3}$. It follows that on the unit circle centered at the origin in the xy-plane, the angle $\frac{92π}{3}$ is the result of $15$ revolutions from its starting side on the positive x-axis followed by a rotation through $\frac{2π}{3}$ radians. Therefore, the angles $\frac{92π}{3}$ and $\frac{2π}{3}$ are coterminal angles and $\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)$ is equal to $\tan\left(\frac{2π}{3}\right)$. Since $\frac{2π}{3}$ is greater than $\frac{π}{2}$ and less than $π$, it follows that the terminal side of the angle is in quadrant II and forms an angle of $\frac{π}{3}$, or $60^\circ$, with the negative x-axis. Therefore, the terminal side of the angle intersects the unit circle at the point $\left(-\frac{1}{2},\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$. It follows that the value of $\tan\left(\frac{2π}{3}\right)$ is $\frac{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}{-\frac{1}{2}}$, which is equivalent to $-\sqrt{3}$. Therefore, the value of $\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)$ is $-\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $\frac{1}{\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)}$, not $\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. This is the value of $\frac{1}{\tan\left(\frac{π}{3}\right)}$, not $\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $\tan\left(\frac{π}{3}\right)$, not $\tan\left(\frac{92π}{3}\right)$.
